2604.14746.title: Disentangle-then-Refine: LLM-Guided Decoupling and Structure-Aware Refinement for Graph Contrastive Learning
2604.14746.paper_summary: Conventional Graph Contrastive Learning (GCL) on Text-Attributed Graphs (TAGs) relies on blind stochastic augmentations, inadvertently entangling task-relevant signals with noise. We propose SDM-SCR, a robust framework anchored in Approximate Orthogonal Decomposition. First, the Semantic Decoupling Module (SDM) leverages the instruction-following capability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to actively parse raw attributes into asymmetric, task-oriented signal and noise views. This shifts the paradigm from random perturbation to semantic-aware disentanglement. Subsequently, Semantic Consistency Regularization (SCR) exploits the spectral observation that semantic signals are topologically smooth while residual noise is high-frequency. SCR functions as a selective spectral filter, enforcing consistency only on the signal subspace to eliminate LLM hallucinations without over-smoothing. This ``Disentangle-then-Refine'' mechanism ensures rigorous signal purification. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SDM-SCR achieves SOTA performance in accuracy and efficiency.
2604.14746.paper_summary_zh: 傳統的文本屬性圖（TAGs）上的圖對比學習（GCL）依賴於盲目的隨機增強，無意中將任務相關信號與噪聲糾纏在一起。我們提出了SDM-SCR，一個基於近似正交分解的穩健框架。首先，語義解耦模塊（SDM）利用大型語言模型（LLMs）的指令跟隨能力，主動將原始屬性解析為不對稱的、以任務為導向的信號和噪聲視圖。這將範式從隨機擾動轉變為語義意識的解耦。隨後，語義一致性正則化（SCR）利用光譜觀察，發現語義信號在拓撲上是平滑的，而殘餘噪聲則是高頻的。SCR作為一種選擇性光譜濾波器，僅在信號子空間上強制一致性，以消除LLM的幻覺，而不會過度平滑。這種“解耦-然後-精煉”的機制確保了嚴格的信號純化。大量實驗表明，SDM-SCR在準確性和效率上達到了SOTA性能。
2604.14746.author: Zhaoxing Li et.al.
2604.14746.authors: Zhaoxing Li, Hai-Feng Zhang, Xiaoming Zhang
